title: Salesforce sync truncates international phone country codes longer than 2 digits
problem: The nightly Salesforce-to-TaskFlow customer sync is truncating international phone number country codes that are longer than 2 digits. For example, Irish numbers with country code +353 are stored as +35, making them invalid. An estimated 50-100 of ~2,000 customer records are affected. US (+1) and UK (+44) numbers are not affected because their country codes are 1-2 digits.
root_cause_hypothesis: A recent change (within the last ~2 weeks) in the Salesforce sync import pipeline is truncating the country code portion of phone numbers to 2 digits. This could be a database column length constraint, a parsing/formatting bug in the sync code that assumes all country codes are 1-2 digits, or a regex/split operation that mishandles 3-digit country codes like +353, +354, +355, etc.
reproduction_steps: Identify a customer record in Salesforce with a 3-digit country code (e.g., +353 for Ireland, +886 for Taiwan)
Verify the phone number is correct and complete in Salesforce
Wait for the nightly sync to run (or trigger it manually)
Check the same record in TaskFlow — the country code should be truncated to 2 digits
Compare against a US or UK record to confirm those are unaffected
environment: Salesforce-to-TaskFlow nightly sync integration; affects international phone numbers with 3+ digit country codes
impact: Support team cannot reach ~50-100 international customers by phone. Data integrity issue that worsens with each nightly sync as more records are corrupted. Existing corrupted records will need a backfill repair.
recommended_fix: 1. Check git/deploy history for changes to the Salesforce sync code or phone number schema in the last 2-3 weeks. 2. Review the phone number parsing/storage logic — look for field length constraints, substring operations, or regex patterns that assume country codes are at most 2 digits. 3. Fix the parsing to handle country codes of 1-3 digits (per ITU E.164 standard). 4. Run a one-time backfill by re-syncing affected records from Salesforce to repair corrupted numbers. 5. Add a validation check that flags phone numbers whose country code prefix doesn't match a known valid code.
proposed_test_case: Create test phone numbers with country codes of varying lengths (+1, +44, +353, +1684) and run them through the sync import pipeline. Assert that all numbers are stored exactly as provided, with no truncation. Include edge cases like +1 (1-digit), +44 (2-digit), +353 (3-digit), and numbers with/without the + prefix.
information_gaps: Exact date the truncation started (reporter estimates ~2 weeks but is unsure)
Whether a specific deployment or config change to the sync coincided with the onset
Exact before/after phone number examples from the database (reporter described the pattern but didn't have records in front of them)
